records.ety: [F. rpugnant, or L. repugnans, -antis, p. pr. of repugnare. See Repugn.]

defs: Disposed to fight against; hostile; at war with; being at variance; contrary; inconsistent; refractory; disobedient; also, distasteful in a high degree; offensive; -- usually followed by to, rarely and less properly by with; as, all rudeness was repugnant to her nature.
quotes: There is no breach of a divine law but is more or less repugnant unto the will of the Lawgiver, God himself.
